mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, invalidating the labor deterioration channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in residential and consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds the 110 index level, robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization drive immediate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when industrial production remains above 110 for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 205 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which boosts corporate profit margins and consumer confidence, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over any 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation (Rank IC = 0.00) with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 105 index threshold indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which force immediate earnings downgrades and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates false negatives in >70% of cases where industrial production drops below 105 but equity markets rally due to liquidity injections.
